name: github-code-review
description: Comprehensive GitHub code review with AI-powered swarm coordination
capabilities: Deploys specialized AI agents for security, performance, style, and architecture reviews
Analyzes pull requests using GitHub CLI integration
Generates contextual review comments with severity levels
Supports custom review agents and quality gate configuration
Integrates with GitHub Actions for automated review workflows
author: Claude Code Flow
securitySummary: This skill contains ONLY markdown documentation. No executable code, no file system operations, no network operations, and no credential access. All external command references are within documentation examples only. The static scanner flagged pattern matches in bash examples and security documentation concepts, but these are illustrative examples for legitimate GitHub CLI and ruv-swarm tool usage. No malicious intent or actual security risk present.
valueStatement: Code reviews require manual effort and may miss security issues or performance problems. This skill coordinates multiple AI agents to review pull requests across security, performance, style, and architecture dimensions simultaneously.
userTitle: Review code with AI swarm agents
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) and ruv-swarm tools to be installed
Cannot execute code or run tests directly
Review quality depends on AI model capabilities and context provided
Labels and comments require appropriate GitHub permissions

useCases.title: Multi-agent security review
useCases.description: Deploy security-focused AI agents to analyze pull requests for vulnerabilities before merging

useCases.title: Automated PR quality gates
useCases.description: Set up automated review workflows that check code quality, style, and documentation standards

useCases.title: CI/CD review integration
useCases.description: Integrate AI-powered code review into GitHub Actions pipelines for continuous quality assurance

promptTemplates.title: Basic PR review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the github-code-review skill to review PR 123 for security issues and code quality. Post the results as a comment.

promptTemplates.title: Security-focused review
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a security-only review on PR 456 using the security review agent. Check for OWASP vulnerabilities and suggest fixes.

promptTemplates.title: Performance analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze PR 789 with the performance review agent. Identify bottlenecks and suggest optimizations with benchmarks.

promptTemplates.title: Complete swarm review
promptTemplates.prompt: Initiate a comprehensive review on PR 321 using security, performance, style, and architecture agents. Post all findings to the PR.

bestPractices: Configure quality gates with clear thresholds for blocking merges on critical issues
Use label-based agent assignment to route PRs to appropriate specialized reviewers
Combine AI reviews with human oversight for final approval decisions
antiPatterns: Relying solely on AI reviews without human verification of critical changes
Ignoring severity classifications and treating all issues as equally important
Running reviews on very large PRs without incremental or focused review modes

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Each may have different review capabilities.

faq.question: What are the rate limits for GitHub API calls?
faq.answer: GitHub API limits apply to all operations. The skill uses gh CLI which handles rate limit errors gracefully.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with existing CI/CD tools?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes GitHub Actions workflow examples that integrate with any CI/CD pipeline.

faq.question: Is my code data sent to external services?
faq.answer: Code is processed locally by AI tools you configure. Only GitHub API calls are made to your repository.

faq.question: Why are no review comments appearing on my PR?
faq.answer: Check GitHub token permissions (needs repo scope), verify gh CLI authentication, and review API rate limits.

faq.question: How does this compare to GitHub Copilot code review?
faq.answer: This skill coordinates multiple specialized agents for comprehensive review. Copilot provides inline suggestions during coding.
